key_rights: Right to access information held by public authorities.
Right to ask for and receive information from any public authority.
Right to inspect government records, documents, memos, and emails.
Right to take notes, extracts, or certified copies of information.
Right to request information in electronic formats (e.g., diskettes, printouts).
Right to receive information within 30 days (or 48 hours if concerning life or liberty).
Right to appeal a denied request to a senior officer.
Right to appeal to the Information Commission if a request is denied.
important_provisions: Promotes transparency and accountability in the working of all public authorities.
Aims to reduce corruption and ensure government agencies are answerable to the people.
Public Information Officers (PIOs) or Assistant PIOs are appointed in every government office.
PIOs must provide information within 30 days (or 48 hours for life/liberty matters).
Public authorities are obligated to proactively publish certain information online and in public.
Information can be denied if it affects national security, sovereignty, or integrity of India.
Information can be denied if it endangers someone's life or impedes investigations.
Information can be denied if it involves personal information that would be an unwarranted invasion of privacy (unless there's a larger public interest).
Information related to commercial confidence, trade secrets, or cabinet papers can be withheld under certain conditions.
Certain intelligence and security organizations are generally exempt, except for information related to human rights violations.
important_penalties: A PIO can face a penalty of Rs. 250 per day (up to a maximum of Rs. 25,000) for failing to provide information on time, maliciously denying a request, giving incorrect/incomplete information, or obstructing the process.
The Central or State Information Commission can recommend disciplinary action against the defaulting officer.
